title: Problems creating a podman machine on WSL that uses user mode networking
body:
### Issue Description

Installing Podman and creating the default machine WITH user mode networking enabled leads to commands that need dns resolution not working such as but not limited to podman compose up --build. 


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
-1. set wsl to mirrored mode.
0. delete podman and "unregister" old podman distros if you had podman installed and running already.
1. install podman.
2. go through the initial setup.
3. go to resources and create a podman machine WITH user mode networking enabled.
4. after podman toasts that it is done open windows terminal and podman-machine-default and podman-net-usermode distros should be created.
5. connect to a vpn.
6. attach to podman-machine-default:

```
[user@computer~]$ ping google.com
^C --> not working
[user@computer~]$ cat /etc/resolv.conf

nameserver 192.168.127.1
[user@computer~]$ ls -l /etc/resolv.conf
--> not a symlink
[user@computer~]$ cat /etc/wsl.conf
[user]
default=user

[network]
generateResolvConf = false
```



### Describe the results you received

Two distros were created:

podman-machine-default
-> can not resolve dns when host is connected to vpn.

podman-net-usermode
-> can resolve dns when host is connected to vpn.
BUT:
podman-net-usermode is apparently not used.

### Describe the results you expected

That podman actually uses the second working distro somehow OR
That only podman-machine-default is created but with a generated resolv.conf (this works on all other wsl distros and does not break when the host is connected to a vpn).

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

WSL is in mirrored mode.

### Additional information

I can fix the setup by setting `generateResolvConf = true`, removing `/etc/resolv.conf` and restarting the wsl.

But what is the point about giving me a setting when it does not work? And what is the point of the podman-net-usermode WSL distro if I have to reconfigure podman-machine-default to make it work?

comments.body: I had the same error. I was able to fix it by copying over the /etc/resolve.conf from podman-net-usermode to the one in podman-default-machine (i.e. replacing the one in the default machine with the one in user mode). I believe this requires you to be rootless.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> I had the same error. I was able to fix it by copying over the /etc/resolve.conf from podman-net-usermode to the one in podman-default-machine (i.e. replacing the one in the default machine with the one in user mode). I believe this requires you to be rootless.

Even if this works, users should not have to do that.

Same issue, but I don't think the VPN is required to reproduce. Usermode networking appears broken even off the VPN. The usermode container is working fine both on and off VPN for me but the default machine is completely broken when trying to resolve using 192.168.127.1. I'm running rootless and usermode networking (obviously). This issue began after the latest upgrade from 5.7.x to 5.8.2. I've removed and recreated the Podman machines but no luck.

Edit: I see this is related/dup #28609
